title: Demo Walkthrough

# Demo Walkthrough — Azure Monitor & Application Insights

A suggested ~15-minute presentation flow once the environment is deployed via the
[CLI path](cli/README.md) or [portal path](portal/README.md). All telemetry shown is
**real** — generated by exercising the deployed app, never fabricated (constitution:
Honesty & Source Fidelity).

## 0. Generate traffic

The deployed Functions app already calls the web app on a 5-minute timer. To create
fresh, on-demand activity during the demo:

```powershell
# Pass the real Web App URL printed by deploy.ps1 (the script's built-in default is a
# placeholder from the upstream author and will NOT match your deployment).
cd azure-monitor-demo
./scripts/generate-traffic.ps1 -AppUrl "https://<your-webapp>.azurewebsites.net"
```

This issues ~30 requests randomly across the real endpoints:
`/`, `/api/health`, `/api/products`, `/api/simulate-error`, `/api/load-test`,
`/api/memory-test`. The `simulate-error` / `load-test` / `memory-test` endpoints
intentionally produce failures, slow responses, and high memory usage so the
monitoring views have something to show.

## 1. Live Metrics (real-time)

Application Insights → **Live Metrics**. Show incoming request rate, failures, and
server health updating in real time as the traffic script runs.

## 2. Application Map

Application Insights → **Application Map**. Show the app node and its dependency on the
**SQL database** and any outbound calls, with success/failure rates on the edges.

## 3. Failures

Application Insights → **Failures**. Drill into the failed `/api/simulate-error`
requests; open an end-to-end transaction to show the exception detail and stack trace.

## 4. Performance

Application Insights → **Performance**. Show response-time distribution; the
`/api/load-test` and `/api/memory-test` endpoints surface as the slow operations.

## 5. Logs (KQL)

Application Insights → **Logs**. Run a couple of queries against the real data:

```kql
requests
| summarize count(), avg(duration) by name
| order by count_ desc
```

```kql
exceptions
| summarize count() by type, outerMessage
| order by count_ desc
```

## 6. Alerts

Show the two metric alerts created by the template (**High Response Time** > 5 s,
**High Error Rate** > 10%) under the resource group → **Alerts** / **Alert rules**.
Under sustained load-test/error traffic these can fire, demonstrating proactive
monitoring.

## 7. Teardown

End the demo by deleting everything to control cost (constitution: Reproducible
Teardown & Cost Control):

```powershell
az group delete --name demo-monitor-rg --yes --no-wait
```

See [cost notes](cost.md) for an approximate running-cost estimate.
